content.text: Answer 4 questions. Indicate which 4 problems you wish to be graded. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote theorems (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problem.text: Let \(p\) be a prime and \(G\) be a nontrivial finite \(p\)-group.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that every nontrivial finite \(p\)-group has a normal subgroup of index \(p\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(\sigma\in A_n\) if and only if \(n\) is odd.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Prove that when \(n\) is odd, \(A_n\) has two conjugacy classes of \(n\)-cycles.

problem.text: Let \(G\) be a group and let \(T(G)=\{x\in G:\exists n\geq 1, x^n=1\}\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(G\) is abelian then \(T(G)\) is a subgroup of \(G\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of a nonabelian group \(G\) such that \(T(G)\) is not a subgroup of \(G\). Justify any claims that you make.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Find the order of the group \(\operatorname{GL}(4,\mathbb{Z}/3\mathbb{Z})\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that there exists a nonabelian group of order \(2025=3^4\cdot 5^2\) in which the Sylow \(3\)-subgroup is abelian.

problem.text: Let \(K\) and \(H\) be groups, with \(K\) abelian. Consider a homomorphism \(\phi:K\to\operatorname{Aut}(H)\) and the associated semidirect product \(G=H\rtimes_\phi K\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that when \(K\) is identified with the subgroup \(\{(1,k)\mid k\in K\}\) of \(G\), \(\operatorname{Ker}(\phi)\) is contained in the center of \(G\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that every group of order \(44\) has a cyclic subgroup of order \(22\).

problem.text: Let \(G\) be a finite group, and let \(P\) be a Sylow \(p\)-subgroup of \(G\) for some prime \(p\). Let \(N=N_G(P)\), the normalizer of \(P\). Prove that \(N_G(N)=N\).
